execution_policy.reason: Deterministic document-readback evals; repeated identical passes would add no signal.

prompt_pack.reason: The default self-test pack is autoresearch-skill-specific; deep-interview needs domain-matched prompts.

prompts.text: 이거 바로 만들지 말고 질문으로 요구사항부터 좁혀줘.
Don't assume. Interview me one question at a time before we build this feature.
Fix the null error in src/auth/session.ts.
Give me lots of product ideas for a habit app.
I already know the exact files, constraints, and tests. Do we still need a deep interview?

eval_definitions.name: Trigger boundary
eval_definitions.question: Is it clear whether the prompt should trigger deep-interview, route away, or stop early?

eval_definitions.name: Next action clarity
eval_definitions.question: Does the core skill make the immediate next behavior obvious for this prompt class?

eval_definitions.name: One-question discipline
eval_definitions.question: Would the skill still enforce one-question-at-a-time interviewing instead of batching?

eval_definitions.name: Ambiguity and brownfield gate
eval_definitions.question: Does the core preserve weakest-dimension targeting, ambiguity gating, and repository-first brownfield behavior?

eval_definitions.name: Artifact and handoff clarity
eval_definitions.question: Are `.hypercore` outputs and non-implementation handoff behavior clear?

experiments.description: Initial deep-interview skill baseline before mutation
Add explicit route-away defaults and self-contained support-file guidance
